page: Amazon Web Services i2.2xlarge is a storage-optimized second-generation virtual server instance running on the Xen hypervisor. It features an x86_64 architecture powered by an Intel Xeon E5-2670 v2 processor with 8 dedicated vCPUs (4 physical cores with 2 threads per core) operating at 2.5 GHz. The instance is equipped with 61.0 GB of system memory, providing 7.62 GB per core, and includes a bundled 1600 GB local NVMe SSD storage array. Network connectivity is configured with a baseline bandwidth of 1 Gbps.

Benchmark evaluations indicate average performance across several key metrics, alongside some notable limitations. The Intel Xeon processor delivers average results in stress-ng single-core (1619.98) and multi-core (7138.99) tests, as well as in PassMark CPU (8839.65) and database operations (3356.02). However, memory bandwidth is mixed, showing average read-only speeds for large blocks (40.1 GB/sec) but weak write performance (19.3 GB/sec) and weak cached small-block reads. Single-core processing is a bottleneck, as evidenced by poor Geekbench single-core (529.0) and bzip3 decompression scores. Cryptographic tasks benefit from hardware-accelerated AES support, but advanced vector extensions are limited to legacy AVX, lacking AVX-512 capabilities.

From a cost-efficiency perspective, the i2.2xlarge offers a high density of local NVMe SSD storage and memory relative to its compute resources, making it a viable option for legacy storage-heavy workloads without requiring premium modern compute pricing. However, the older processor architecture and weak single-threaded performance present a tradeoff for compute-intensive tasks. This instance is best suited for database operations, static web serving, and localized data processing workloads that require high-capacity, low-latency local storage rather than high-performance CPU throughput.
description: Amazon Web Services i2.2xlarge is a storage-optimized, second-generation virtual server powered by an Intel Xeon E5-2670 v2 processor. It features 8 dedicated vCPUs, 61.0 GB of memory, and a bundled 1600 GB local NVMe SSD. Operating on the Xen hypervisor with an x86_64 architecture, the instance delivers a baseline network bandwidth of 1 Gbps. Performance benchmarks show average multi-core CPU capabilities and database operations, though single-core performance and memory write bandwidth are relatively weak. Lacking AVX-512 and modern instruction sets, it relies on legacy AVX and AES support. The qualitative cost profile favors workloads requiring high local storage density and moderate memory over raw compute power. It is best suited for database hosting, static web serving, and storage-intensive applications that benefit from direct-attached NVMe SSDs.
og_description: Amazon Web Services i2.2xlarge is a storage-optimized server with 8 vCPUs, 61 GB RAM, and a 1600 GB local NVMe SSD, designed for database workloads and static web serving on the Xen hypervisor.
meta_description: Amazon Web Services i2.2xlarge is a storage-optimized instance featuring 8 vCPUs, 61 GB RAM, and a 1600 GB local NVMe SSD on the Xen hypervisor.
tagline: A storage-optimized virtual server featuring high-capacity local NVMe SSD storage and balanced memory for database and web serving workloads.
bullet_points: 8 dedicated vCPUs powered by an Intel Xeon E5-2670 v2 processor
61.0 GB of system memory paired with a 1600 GB local NVMe SSD
1 Gbps baseline network bandwidth running on the Xen hypervisor
Average performance in database operations and multi-core CPU tasks
Suited for storage-heavy workloads, database hosting, and static web serving
